epic_title: Conflict Coach v1 — Full-Stack AI Mediation Web App
planning_assumptions: The invitee's pre-join display name (typed by initiator at case creation as 'What's their name?') is not in the spec's cases/create mutation input. We add inviteeName: v.optional(v.string()) to the cases table and pass it through cases/create.
Solo mode isSolo=true pre-binds the same userId to both initiator and invitee slots; partyStates has two rows for the same userId with different roles. The party toggle filters by role, not userId.
The spec's COST_LIMITED status is not defined in the schema's case status union. It is added to cases.status in T2.
cases.tokenCount: v.optional(v.number()) is added to the cases table in T2 for AI cost tracking.
Convex Auth is the chosen auth provider (TQ1). The Clerk swap path is documented in README but not implemented.
Admin role promotion is a manual Convex Dashboard action in v1; no self-serve admin claim UI is built.
Typing indicators ('Jordan is typing...') reserve visual space per the design doc but Convex Presence logic is deferred to v1.1 per tech spec §8.3.
The decline-invite path sets the case to CLOSED_ABANDONED per design doc §4.3; added to state machine in T8.
The /cases/:id/invite post-create screen is a distinct route; invitee display name comes from inviteeName.
P2 user stories (US-16 email notifications, US-17 voluntary sharing, US-18 PDF export, US-19 inline rephrase) are explicitly out of scope.
ToS and privacy policy are static placeholder /terms and /privacy routes, not full tasks; linked from auth screens and case creation.

Cost tracking: tokenCount is accumulated per AI action call. Soft cap $2 ≈ 600k Sonnet tokens (hardcoded pricing constant); hard cap $10. Pricing constant is isolated in a single config file for easy update.

T17 budget enforcement logic and error-handling hardening is designed to run after T12-T15 as a cross-cutting enhancement. Token accumulation ACs are embedded in T12-T15 themselves; T17 adds the cap enforcement, degradation behavior, and Anthropic error handling.
dependency_graph_notes: Four strata: (1) Infrastructure [T1-T2], (2) Core modules [T3-T11]: auth, authorization, privacy filter, prompts, compression, state machine are parallel after T2+T4; templates backend (T11) unblocks case creation (T9); (3) Feature backends [T12-T17]: private coaching → synthesis → joint chat entry mutation (T14a) → joint chat coach (T14b) → draft coach; closure parallel after T14a; cost tracking (T17) is a cross-cutting hardening pass after T12-T15; (4) Frontend [T18-T32]: design tokens + shared components (T19a, T19b) → all views. T33 (accessibility) and T34 (E2E) both depend on T30+T32 (all feature UI complete) but NOT on each other — they can run in parallel. T35 (unit tests) depends only on T5+T6+T7+T8 and can run very early. T36 (CI) waits for T33+T34+T35.

tasks.title: Project scaffolding and cold-start setup
tasks.summary: Initialize the project with Vite + React 18 + TypeScript, Tailwind v3, shadcn/ui, React Router v6, Convex client, ESLint + Prettier. Create .env.example, .gitignore, and README with the exact cold-start sequence from tech spec §11.3. Verify `git clone && npm install && npx convex dev && npm run dev` produces a running empty React app.
acceptance_criteria: `npm install` completes without errors on a clean checkout
`npx convex dev` starts without errors (Convex project configured)
`npm run dev` serves a React app at localhost:5173 with no console errors
ESLint and Prettier configs present; `npm run lint` and `npm run format:check` pass on the empty project
`tsc --noEmit` passes with strict mode enabled
README contains verbatim the cold-start sequence from tech spec §11.3
.env.example includes all env vars from §11.2: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_MOCK, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET, SITE_URL, MAGIC_LINK_EMAIL_FROM, VITE_CONVEX_URL, VITE_SITE_URL
stack_practices: Bootstrap with `npm create vite@latest -- --template react-ts`; do NOT use CRA.

Install Convex client: `npm install convex`; init project: `npx convex dev` (first run prompts project creation). The generated `convex/_generated/` directory must be gitignored — it is recreated on every `npx convex dev` run.
Tailwind v3 setup: `npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer && npx tailwindcss init -p`. Content glob must include `./src/**/*.{ts,tsx}` and `./convex/**/*.ts`.
shadcn/ui init: `npx shadcn-ui@latest init`. Choose 'New York' style, CSS variables color strategy. This writes `src/components/ui/` and modifies `tailwind.config.js` — commit both.
React Router v6: `npm install react-router-dom`. Wrap app in `<BrowserRouter>` in main.tsx. Never use React Router v5 patterns (`<Switch>`, `component=` prop).
Wire Convex in main.tsx: `<ConvexProvider client={convex}><App /></ConvexProvider>` — Convex Auth adds `<ConvexAuthProvider>` wrapping `<ConvexProvider>`.
tsconfig.json: enable `strict: true`, `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true`. These are locked — do not soften.
ESLint: use `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` and `eslint-plugin-react-refresh`. Prettier: use `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` for class sorting.
Anti-pattern: do NOT call `npx convex deploy` during dev scaffolding — `npx convex dev` is the development command; deploy is for production.

tasks.title: Convex schema definition (all tables, all fields)
tasks.summary: Implement convex/schema.ts exactly as specified in tech spec §3.1 plus planning-assumption additions.
acceptance_criteria: All 10 tables match tech spec §3.1 field names, types, and union literals exactly
cases.status union includes COST_LIMITED in addition to the 7 spec values
cases table includes: inviteeName: v.optional(v.string()), tokenCount: v.optional(v.number())
partyStates table includes: closureProposalSummary: v.optional(v.string())
All indexes present
`npx convex dev` applies the schema without errors
All 6 key invariants from §3.2 documented as inline comments
`tsc --noEmit` passes
stack_practices: Use `defineSchema` and `defineTable` from `convex/server`; use `v` from `convex/values` for all field validators.
ID references use `v.id('tableName')` not `v.string()`.
Union literals use `v.union(v.literal('A'), v.literal('B'))`.
Anti-pattern: do NOT add `v.any()` fields except where explicitly needed (auditLog.metadata).
After editing schema.ts, `npx convex dev` must be running to push the schema.
The `schemaVersion: v.literal(1)` pattern on `cases` is intentional.

tasks.title: Authentication — Convex Auth with magic link and Google OAuth
tasks.summary: Configure Convex Auth with email magic link and Google OAuth. On first login, upsert a users row.
acceptance_criteria: User registers and logs in via magic link
User logs in via Google OAuth
First login creates a users row with role='USER'
Subsequent logins upsert the users row
Session persists across browser reload
Session expires after 30 days of inactivity
ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() returns the identity in any Convex function after login
stack_practices: Install: `npm install @convex-dev/auth @auth/core`.
Auth config file is `convex/auth.config.ts`.
Magic link provider: use `Resend` email provider.
Google OAuth: requires `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET` in Convex environment variables.
Client side: import `useAuthActions` from `@convex-dev/auth/react`.
Anti-pattern: do NOT store auth tokens in localStorage manually.
ConvexAuthProvider must wrap ConvexProvider in main.tsx.

tasks.title: Authorization helper module and ConvexError codes
tasks.summary: Create convex/lib/auth.ts with requireAuth(ctx) helper, assertPartyToCase, assertAdmin. Define all 9 ConvexError codes. Create convex/lib/pricing.ts.
acceptance_criteria: requireAuth(ctx) returns { identity, user } or throws ConvexError UNAUTHENTICATED
All 9 error codes typed as constants
ConvexError shape: { code, message, httpStatus }
assertPartyToCase throws FORBIDDEN if caller not party
assertAdmin throws FORBIDDEN if user.role !== 'ADMIN'
convex/lib/pricing.ts exports soft/hard caps and per-token pricing
stack_practices: Import `ConvexError` from `convex/values`.
Helper function signature: `async function requireAuth(ctx): Promise<{ identity; user }>`.
File location: `convex/lib/auth.ts` and `convex/lib/pricing.ts`.
Anti-pattern: do NOT put authorization logic inline in each function.

tasks.title: Privacy response filter module
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/privacyFilter.ts: detect ≥8 consecutive whitespace-token substring matches.
acceptance_criteria: filterResponse(response, otherPartyMessages): { passed, matchedSubstring? }
passed=false on 8+ consecutive token window match
Unit tests covering boundary cases and performance
stack_practices: Pure TypeScript module — no Convex runtime imports.
Tokenize by whitespace: `text.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean)`.
Pre-compute N-gram windows into a Set<string> for O(1) lookup.
Function must be synchronous.

tasks.title: Prompt assembly module for all four AI roles
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/prompts.ts with assemblePrompt(opts) for PRIVATE_COACH, SYNTHESIS, COACH, DRAFT_COACH.
acceptance_criteria: assemblePrompt({ role }) returns { system, messages }
All four prompts contain the no-quote rule
SYNTHESIS produces strict JSON instruction
Pure TypeScript, testable in Vitest
stack_practices: Pure TypeScript module — no Convex imports, no SDK imports.
Use multi-line template literals for readability.
SYNTHESIS output format: instruct model to respond with ONLY the JSON object.

tasks.title: Transcript compression module
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/transcriptCompression.ts with content-hash caching and Haiku compression.
acceptance_criteria: compressIfNeeded returns original array when under budget without API call
Over-budget: oldest 50% replaced by SUMMARY message
Content hash caching: same input = one Haiku API call
Token counting uses word-count approximation
stack_practices: Accepts Anthropic client as dependency-injection parameter.
Cache via module-level Map<string, string>.
Use TextEncoder + manual hash loop (no Node crypto module).
Anti-pattern: do NOT call with budgetTokens=0.

tasks.title: Case state machine module (all transitions including decline-invite)
tasks.summary: Implement convex/lib/stateMachine.ts with pure transition functions for all case states.
acceptance_criteria: Each transition is pure: takes state, returns new status or throws CONFLICT
DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING→CLOSED_ABANDONED: invitee decline, explicitly tested
All 9 valid transitions and 8+ illegal transitions tested
stack_practices: Pure TypeScript — zero Convex runtime or SDK imports.
Export one named function per transition.
Anti-pattern: do NOT read from ctx.db inside state machine functions.

tasks.title: Case CRUD backend (create, get, list, partyStates, updateMyForm)
tasks.summary: Implement Convex queries and mutations for case management.
acceptance_criteria: cases/create: creates rows, returns caseId + invite URL
cases/list: returns only caller's cases
cases/get: returns NOT_FOUND if caller not a party
cases/partyStates: returns self (full) and other (phase-only)
stack_practices: File: `convex/cases.ts` exporting query/mutation wrappers.
cases/partyStates privacy: NEVER return synthesisText for the 'other' party.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use `ctx.db.query().collect()` without an index.

tasks.title: Invite flow backend (redeem mutation, preview query, token validation)
tasks.summary: Implement invites/redeem mutation and invites/preview query.
acceptance_criteria: invites/redeem: validates token, atomically binds invitee, marks token CONSUMED
invites/redeem on CONSUMED token: throws TOKEN_INVALID
invites/preview: returns non-private fields only
stack_practices: Atomicity: Convex mutations are fully transactional.
invites/preview is a Convex query — no auth required for preview.
Anti-pattern: do NOT check token validity in invites/preview.

tasks.title: Templates backend and seed data
tasks.summary: Implement template management queries and mutations. Create convex/seed.ts.
acceptance_criteria: templates/list: returns non-archived templates
admin mutations throw FORBIDDEN for non-ADMIN users
convex/seed.ts: creates admin user and three templates
stack_practices: File structure: `convex/templates.ts` and `convex/admin/templates.ts`.
Immutability of templateVersions: enforced by absence of an update mutation.
Anti-pattern: do NOT version templates by mutating the existing row.

tasks.title: Private coaching backend (send message, AI response, mark complete, crisis detection, token tracking)
tasks.summary: Implement private coaching queries, mutations, and AI action.
acceptance_criteria: privateCoaching/myMessages: returns only caller's messages
generateAIResponse: streams into privateMessages row
generateAIResponse: increments cases.tokenCount
privateCoaching/markComplete: idempotent
stack_practices: Streaming pattern: mutation inserts STREAMING row → action streams → flush via internal mutation every ~50ms.
generateAIResponse must be an internalAction.
CLAUDE_MOCK=true: check process.env.CLAUDE_MOCK and return canned response.

tasks.title: AI synthesis backend (generate action, privacy filter, state transition)
tasks.summary: Implement synthesis/generate action: reads both parties' private messages, assembles SYNTHESIS prompt, validates JSON output, runs privacy filter.
acceptance_criteria: Calls claude-sonnet-4-5; validates { forInitiator, forInvitee } JSON
Runs filterResponse on each half; retries up to 2x on failure
On success: writes synthesisText to both partyStates; sets case READY_FOR_JOINT
Idempotent: if synthesisText already populated, returns without re-generating
stack_practices: synthesis/generate is an internalAction only.
Retry loop: for loop 0..2, never use recursion.
Anti-pattern: do NOT call the Anthropic API inside a Convex mutation.

tasks.title: Joint chat entry mutation and Coach facilitation backend
tasks.summary: Implement jointChat/enter, jointChat/messages, jointChat/mySynthesis, jointChat/sendUserMessage, and generateCoachResponse action.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/enter: READY_FOR_JOINT→JOINT_ACTIVE, idempotent
generateCoachResponse: Haiku classifies; Sonnet only for non-NORMAL_EXCHANGE
T5 filter on Coach Sonnet response; fallback on failure
Increments tokenCount; enforces HARD_CAP
stack_practices: Haiku classification: single non-streaming call, max_tokens 50.
Anti-pattern: do NOT hardcode @-mention as a client-side feature — detection happens inside the mutation body.
CLAUDE_MOCK guard: same process.env.CLAUDE_MOCK check as T12.

tasks.title: Draft Coach backend (session lifecycle, AI conversation, readiness detection, send gate)
tasks.summary: Implement draftCoach/session, startSession, sendMessage, generateResponse, sendFinalDraft, discardSession.
acceptance_criteria: draftCoach/startSession: creates draftSessions row; throws CONFLICT if case not JOINT_ACTIVE
Readiness detection: produces { 'draft': '...' } JSON; writes to draftSessions.finalDraft
draftCoach/sendFinalDraft: reads finalDraft; calls jointChat/sendUserMessage internally
Draft Coach conversation invisible to other party
stack_practices: draftCoach/sendFinalDraft calling jointChat/sendUserMessage via ctx.runMutation.
Privacy enforcement: action must NOT read synthesisText for the other party.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use ctx.db.query().collect() to build context — use index with limit.

tasks.title: Case closure backend (propose, confirm, reject, unilateral close, abandoned cron)
tasks.summary: Implement closure mutations and convex/crons.ts daily job for CLOSED_ABANDONED detection.
acceptance_criteria: jointChat/proposeClosure: sets closureProposed; writes to auditLog
jointChat/confirmClosure: atomically closes case as CLOSED_RESOLVED
Cron: daily scan for JOINT_ACTIVE cases with 30d inactivity
stack_practices: Cron setup: `convex/crons.ts` using cronJobs() from convex/server.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use setInterval or external cron services.

tasks.title: AI cost tracking hardening: budget enforcement, soft-cap degradation, Anthropic error handling
tasks.summary: Cross-cutting hardening pass: soft-cap degradation, hard-cap enforcement, Anthropic error handling across all AI actions.
acceptance_criteria: Soft cap: generateCoachResponse posts boilerplate at $2 threshold
Hard cap: all four AI actions return early at $10; set COST_LIMITED
429: retry once after 2000ms; second 429 → RATE_LIMITED
Network timeout >30s: message status=ERROR; throw AI_ERROR
stack_practices: Cap check at the top of each AI action using values from convex/lib/pricing.ts.
Retry: fixed 2s delay, not exponential.
Anti-pattern: do NOT let AI errors bubble up as unhandled Convex action failures.

tasks.title: Design tokens and global styles
tasks.summary: Implement all CSS custom properties from design doc §2.1, Tailwind config mapping, Inter + JetBrains Mono fonts, dark mode via prefers-color-scheme, shadow levels, and motion tokens.
acceptance_criteria: All 16 color tokens defined as CSS custom properties in src/index.css
Dark mode via @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)
Tailwind config maps each token to a semantic utility class
Inter variable font loaded (400/500/600); JetBrains Mono loaded
Four shadow utilities matching design doc values
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce): all transitions 0ms, animations paused
stack_practices: CSS custom properties go in `src/index.css` under `:root` and `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`.
Tailwind config extension maps tokens: `canvas: 'var(--bg-canvas)'` etc.
shadcn/ui CSS variable names must match the shadcn convention.
Tailwind v3 dark mode: set `darkMode: 'media'` in tailwind.config.js.
Anti-pattern: do NOT define color values in both tailwind.config.js and src/index.css separately.
design_practices: Implement all 16 color tokens verbatim from design doc §2.1: --bg-canvas (#FAF8F5), --bg-surface (#FFFFFF), --bg-surface-subtle (#F3EFE9), --text-primary (#1F1D1A), --text-secondary (#5C5952), --text-tertiary (#8A8680), --accent (#6B8E7F), --accent-hover (#5A7A6C), --accent-subtle (#DCE7E0), --danger (#B5594D), --danger-subtle (#F2DCD8), --warning (#B58B4D), --border-default (#E5E0D8), --border-strong (#CBC4B8), --coach-accent (#8B7AB5), --private-tint (#F0E9E0).

Typography (design doc §2.2): Inter variable font at 400/500/600 weights; body text 15px / 1.6 line-height (chat bubbles 16px for readability); chat timestamps 12px in --text-tertiary; headings at 500 weight — NOT 700 (this is not a marketing page); JetBrains Mono for invite links and audit log IDs.

Spacing & Layout (design doc §2.3): 8px base grid with paddings at 8, 16, 24, 32, 48px. Content max-width 720px for reading-heavy screens (forms, synthesis); 1080px for joint chat. Mobile breakpoint: < 768px.

Elevation (design doc §2.4): shadow-0 = flat/borders only; shadow-1 = '0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)' for cards at rest; shadow-2 = '0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)' for popovers/dropdowns; shadow-3 = '0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.10)' for modals and Draft Coach panel.

Motion (design doc §2.6): all transitions 150–200ms ease-out; new message arrival = fade-in + 8px upward translate (150ms); route crossfade 100ms; NO slide animations; NO celebration animation on case closure. Under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce → set all transition durations to 0ms and pause all CSS animations.
Dark mode: invert luminance of all 16 tokens while preserving warm, low-saturation character — no stark white-on-black. Reference design doc §2.1 dark mode note.
Accessibility: ensure every --text-* / --bg-* combination meets WCAG 2.1 AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio in both light and dark modes. Use the are-you-ok CLI or browser DevTools to verify before T33.

tasks.title: Core chat UI components (ChatWindow, MessageBubble, StreamingIndicator, MessageInput)
tasks.summary: Build foundational chat primitives used by all three chat UIs.
acceptance_criteria: ChatWindow auto-scrolls to latest unless user has scrolled up >100px
MessageBubble USER: right-aligned, bg-surface; COACH/AI: left-aligned, accent-subtle, Sparkles icon
MessageBubble STREAMING: content + blinking cursor; no copy button
MessageBubble COMPLETE: copy button + timestamp on hover
MessageBubble ERROR: warning-tint + Retry button
StreamingIndicator: 500ms blink; aria-hidden
MessageInput: Enter sends; Shift+Enter newline; disabled while STREAMING
No accessibility defects: textarea has visible label or aria-label
stack_practices: Component files: src/components/chat/ChatWindow.tsx, MessageBubble.tsx, MessageInput.tsx, StreamingIndicator.tsx. Use named exports.
Auto-scroll: useRef<HTMLDivElement> + useEffect calling scrollTo when messages change, conditional on scroll position check.
shadcn/ui primitives: Button from @/components/ui/button; Lucide icons (Sparkles, Copy, AlertTriangle) from lucide-react.
StreamingIndicator animation: @keyframes blink wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference).
MessageInput keyboard: onKeyDown checks e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use dangerouslySetInnerHTML — use react-markdown with rehype-sanitize.
design_practices: MessageBubble — USER variant: right-aligned, background --bg-surface, no icon. Spacing: 16px horizontal padding, 12px vertical. Max-width ~70% of ChatWindow. Border-radius 12px. Use shadow-0 (flat + border-default).

MessageBubble — COACH/AI variant: left-aligned, background --accent-subtle (#DCE7E0), Sparkles icon (Lucide, 1.5px stroke, 16px) at top-left. Coach messages from joint chat additionally get a 2px left border in --coach-accent (#8B7AB5). Same border-radius and padding as USER variant.

MessageBubble — STREAMING: render blinking cursor (thin 1px vertical bar) at end of streaming text. Blink @keyframe: 0%,100% opacity:1; 50% opacity:0 at 500ms step-end. Wrap in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference). StreamingIndicator must be aria-hidden='true' so screen readers do not repeat it.
MessageBubble — COMPLETE: copy button appears on hover (opacity transition 150ms ease-out). Timestamp rendered below bubble in 12px --text-tertiary. Copy button uses Lucide Copy icon at 14px.
MessageBubble — ERROR: background --danger-subtle (#F2DCD8), Lucide AlertTriangle icon in --danger (#B5594D). Retry button uses shadcn/ui Button variant='ghost' in danger color.

MessageInput textarea: Inter 15px, --text-primary, border --border-strong, focus-visible ring 2px --accent. Send button: --accent fill, disabled state opacity-50. Reserve visual space below input for 'Jordan is typing…' (design doc §4.9, deferred v1.1) — a 20px empty row prevents layout jump.

ChatWindow container: role='log' aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions' per design doc §9.5 and T33. New message arrival animation: fade-in + translateY(-8px → 0) at 150ms ease-out, under prefers-reduced-motion guard.
Accessibility: textarea must have a visible label or aria-label. All icon-only buttons need aria-label (e.g., aria-label='Copy message'). Retry button: role='button' with descriptive aria-label.

tasks.title: Secondary UI components (PrivacyBanner, PartyAvatar, CoachIntervention, PhaseHeader, StatusPill, CrisisCard, skeletons)
tasks.summary: Build remaining shared components.
acceptance_criteria: PrivacyBanner: lock icon + Dialog explaining privacy; screen reader text
PartyAvatar: role-colored circles; shows first initial
CoachIntervention: coach-accent border, ⟡ glyph, accent-subtle background
PhaseHeader: current phase label + Back to Dashboard link
StatusPill: ●○◐◼ with correct color mapping
CrisisCard: appears on crisis keywords; dismissable; links to crisis resources
Dashboard skeleton: 3 case row skeletons
Case detail skeleton: phase-appropriate layout
stack_practices: shadcn/ui Dialog for PrivacyBanner modal; Sheet for My Guidance panel and mobile Draft Coach.
Skeleton components: use shadcn/ui Skeleton primitive with 300ms delay before showing.
CrisisCard: standalone component with isVisible and onDismiss props, local useState for dismissed.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use raw <dialog> HTML elements — use shadcn/ui Dialog (Radix UI).
Screen reader text: use sr-only Tailwind class.
design_practices: PrivacyBanner: persistent strip at top of private-scoped views. Background --private-tint (#F0E9E0). Lucide Lock icon (1.5px stroke, 16px) in --text-secondary. Text: --text-secondary, 14px. Clicking the banner opens a shadcn/ui Dialog (shadow-3 elevation) explaining exactly what is private. Include <span className='sr-only'> with full explanation for screen readers.

PartyAvatar: 32×32px circle (rounded-full). Colors per design doc §4.9: Alex/Initiator = soft blue (#BFDBFE), Jordan/Invitee = soft rose (#FECDD3), Coach = --coach-accent (#8B7AB5) at 30% opacity background with --coach-accent text. First initial, 14px Inter 500 weight, centered. Apply aria-label='[Name] avatar'.

CoachIntervention message (design doc §4.9): left border 2px solid --coach-accent. Background --accent-subtle. ⟡ glyph rendered before coach message text (Unicode U+27C1 or SVG). isIntervention flag adds --warning (#B58B4D) background tint instead of --accent-subtle to signal inflammatory-content response.

PhaseHeader (design doc §3.2): full-width top bar, bg-surface, border-bottom --border-default. Left: '← Back to Dashboard' (ghost link, --text-secondary). Center: 'Case with [Name] • [Phase Label]' in 500 weight, --text-primary. Solo cases: append 'Solo' badge — text-xs, bg-coach-accent/20, text-coach-accent, px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded.

StatusPill (design doc §4.4): ● green (--accent #6B8E7F) = Your Turn; ○ gray (--text-secondary) = Waiting; ◐ amber (--warning #B58B4D) = Ready for Joint; ◼ neutral (--text-tertiary) = Closed. Render glyph + short label. 12px --text-secondary typography.

CrisisCard: bg-danger-subtle (#F2DCD8) border border-danger-subtle, 8px radius, 16px padding. Lucide AlertTriangle in --danger. Heading 'Need immediate help?' in --danger 500 weight. Dismissable via ✕ icon button with aria-label='Dismiss crisis card'. Links styled as --accent underline.

Skeleton components: use shadcn/ui Skeleton (animated bg-surface-subtle shimmer). Dashboard row skeletons: 3 rows at ~72px height each. Show after 300ms delay (useEffect + setTimeout) to avoid skeleton flash on fast loads.

Accessibility (all T19b components): shadcn/ui Dialog and Sheet handle focus trapping via Radix UI — do NOT reimplement. All interactive elements need visible focus ring: 2px solid --accent, offset 2px. PrivacyBanner lock icon button: aria-label='Learn more about your privacy'.

tasks.title: Landing page and login/register page
tasks.summary: Implement / (hero, explainer, footer) and /login (magic link, Google OAuth, states). Wire Convex Auth signIn methods.
acceptance_criteria: / displays tagline, 3-step explainer, privacy section, footer with links
/ has 'Start a case' CTA routing to /login or /dashboard
/login: centered ~400px card; email + magic link button; Google OAuth button
Submitting email triggers magic link; form replaced by confirmation state
Error state inline (not a toast)
Fine print links to /terms and /privacy
After auth: redirect to /dashboard
stack_practices: Magic link submit: `const { signIn } = useAuthActions(); await signIn('resend', { email })`.
Google OAuth: `await signIn('google')`.
Error display: catch errors in try/catch; render inline with role='alert'.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use <form action=...> — handle via onSubmit + e.preventDefault().
design_practices: Landing page tone (design doc §1.1 Calm over Clever): one-sentence tagline ('A calm place to work through a difficult conversation.'), short subhead. No testimonials, no aggressive repeated CTAs, no gamification. Colors --bg-canvas background, --text-primary body. Primary CTA 'Start a case' uses --accent (#6B8E7F) filled button — the sole primary action on the page (design doc §1.4).

Landing 3-step explainer: minimal iconographic layout using Lucide icons (MessageCircle, Users, ShieldCheck at 1.5px stroke). Max-width 720px centered. Section padding 48px vertical. No decorative illustrations — presence over polish (design doc §1.5).
Landing footer: --text-tertiary, 12px, border-top --border-default. Links to /terms and /privacy. Per design doc §4.1, no pricing section.

Login page (design doc §4.2): single centered card ~400px wide, bg-surface, shadow-2 ('0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)'). Background --bg-canvas. Heading 'Sign in to Conflict Coach' at 500 weight, --text-primary. Email Input: border --border-strong, 15px Inter. 'Send magic link' = primary --accent filled button (full width). Divider 'or' in --text-tertiary. 'Continue with Google' = secondary outlined button (full width).
Login error state: inline below triggering input, 14px --danger (#B5594D), role='alert' for screen reader announcement. NOT a toast — per design doc §6.2.
Login magic-link-sent state: replace form with confirmation message in --text-secondary. 'Check your email…' — no animation celebrating it.
Fine print: 12px --text-tertiary below both buttons. Links to /terms and /privacy underlined in --accent.

Accessibility: single <main> landmark on both pages. Login card inputs must have <label> elements. Focus visible ring 2px --accent. Tab order: email → send button → divider → Google button → fine print links. Route change announces h1 to screen readers (tabIndex={-1} on h1, per T33 pattern).

tasks.title: Invite acceptance page (/invite/:token)
tasks.summary: Implement invite acceptance per design doc §4.3. Handles logged-out, logged-in unredeemed, consumed, and non-existent token states.
acceptance_criteria: Logged-out: explanation card + 'Sign in to continue'; token preserved through auth
Logged-in unredeemed: shows initiator name, mainTopic (labeled), category; no private content
Accept calls invites/redeem; routes to /cases/:id/private
Decline → CLOSED_ABANDONED → redirect with toast
CONSUMED token: 'This invite has already been used.'
Non-existent token: NOT_FOUND error message
stack_practices: Route param: `const { token } = useParams<{ token: string }>()`.
Token persistence through auth: store in sessionStorage before redirecting to /login.
Preview query: `const preview = useQuery(api.invites.preview, { token: token! })`.
Toast: use shadcn/ui toast. Anti-pattern: do NOT call invites/redeem on page load.
design_practices: Page layout (design doc §4.3): centered card on --bg-canvas, max-width ~480px, bg-surface shadow-1. All states use this same card container.

Logged-out state: heading 'Alex has invited you to work through something together.' (500 weight, --text-primary). Body text in --text-secondary 15px per the design doc's exact copy. Single primary button 'Sign in to continue' (--accent). No decorative imagery.

Logged-in unredeemed state: show initiator's mainTopic labeled 'Alex wrote this in the shared summary.' Helper text: 'You'll have your own private space to share your perspective.' in --text-secondary 14px. Category pill: --bg-surface-subtle, --text-secondary, 12px. Lock icon (Lucide) adjacent to the privacy callout — privacy must be visible, not implied (design doc §1.3).
Accept button: primary --accent, full width within the card. Decline: ghost or text-only link in --text-secondary to visually subordinate it — 'One primary action per screen' (design doc §1.4).
CONSUMED / NOT_FOUND states: --text-secondary body text, no alarming colors. Offer 'Go to dashboard' secondary link.
Privacy label styling: use --private-tint (#F0E9E0) background on the section that shows the main topic, reinforcing that this is the only non-private data shared. ShieldCheck Lucide icon adjacent.

Accessibility: single <main>. Heading hierarchy h1 → h2. Accept/Decline buttons have clear accessible text. Error states use role='alert'. Toast (decline confirmation) announced by screen reader via shadcn/ui toast's built-in aria-live.

tasks.title: Dashboard page (/dashboard) with unilateral-close notification indicator
tasks.summary: Implement /dashboard: Active/Closed sections, case rows with status glyphs, Nudge button, empty state, [+ New Case] button, and notification indicator for unilateral closes.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches cases/list; renders skeleton while loading
Active/Closed sections with correct status glyph mapping
Unilateral close indicator: ◼ with red dot + message for CLOSED_UNRESOLVED non-actor
Nudge button on DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING cases >7 days old without invitee
Empty state with link to /cases/new
Case rows keyboard-navigable; Enter activates
stack_practices: Data fetching: `const cases = useQuery(api.cases.list)` — reactive, no polling needed.
Closed section default collapsed; auto-expand when case transitions to CLOSED_*.
React Router: case row click → navigate('/cases/${caseId}'). Keyboard: role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown Enter.
Anti-pattern: do NOT derive status glyph logic in JSX directly — extract a getStatusGlyph() helper.
design_practices: Page layout: --bg-canvas background. Top-right: '+ New Case' primary --accent button (design doc §4.4). Max-width content wrapper with 32px horizontal padding at desktop, 16px on mobile.
Section headings 'Active Cases' / 'Closed Cases': 500 weight, --text-primary, 16px. 'Closed Cases' collapsed by default with a ChevronDown icon (Lucide 1.5px stroke).

Case row (design doc §4.4): bg-surface, shadow-1 on hover (transition 150ms ease-out), border-bottom --border-default between rows. Padding 16px. Layout: icon column left → case title + metadata stack → status glyph + 'Enter →' right.
Case title: 15px 500 weight --text-primary. Sub-metadata (category · date): 12px --text-secondary. Status line: 13px --text-secondary with glyph. Last activity: 12px --text-tertiary.

Status glyphs (design doc §4.4): ● --accent green = Your Turn; ○ --text-secondary gray = Waiting; ◐ --warning amber = Ready for Joint; ◼ --text-tertiary neutral = Closed. Unilateral close: ◼ with a 6px --danger dot overlay + 'Jordan closed this case.' in 13px --danger.
Nudge button: shadcn/ui Button variant='outline', 13px, --text-secondary. Appears on stale DRAFT_PRIVATE_COACHING rows without invitee, >7 days old.
Empty state: centered in content area, --text-secondary 15px, friendly non-cutesy copy per design doc §4.4. Link to /cases/new in --accent underline.
Skeletons: 3 case row skeletons at 72px height with 300ms delay. Use shadcn/ui Skeleton with --bg-surface-subtle shimmer.

Accessibility: case rows use role='button' tabIndex={0}. Enter key activates. 'Enter →' link has aria-label='Open case with [name]' to differentiate rows for screen readers. section elements with aria-labelledby for Active/Closed headings.

tasks.title: Case creation form and post-create invite sharing screen
tasks.summary: Implement /cases/new (5-step progressive form) and /cases/:id/invite (share screen).
acceptance_criteria: Five steps with Back/Next navigation; Submit on step 5
Step 1: category radio cards for all 5 categories
Step 2: mainTopic with 140-char counter, labeled 'visible to other person'
Steps 3+4: lock icon + tooltip on private fields
Step 5: other party name; Advanced disclosure with solo mode
Crisis language prominently displayed
Submit calls cases/create; routes to /cases/:id/invite
/cases/:id/invite: copyable invite URL; email mailto pre-fill; 'Start coaching now' link
stack_practices: Multi-step form state: single FormData object with setFormData(prev => ({ ...prev, field: value })).
Character counter: {mainTopic.length}/140; text-danger when >140. Do NOT use maxLength HTML attr.
Radio cards for category: shadcn/ui RadioGroup + RadioGroupItem. NOT a <select> dropdown.
shadcn/ui Tooltip for private field explainer; Collapsible for 'Advanced' section.
Clipboard API: navigator.clipboard.writeText(inviteUrl).
design_practices: Form container: single-column, max-width 720px, bg-canvas, centered. Form card uses bg-surface, shadow-1, 32px padding. Step indicator at top: small pills or numbered steps in --text-secondary with current step in --accent.

Step 1 — Category radio cards (design doc §4.5): each category is a full-width card with border --border-default, bg-surface, 16px padding. Selected state: border 2px --accent, bg-accent-subtle. Hover: bg-surface-subtle, transition 150ms ease-out. Labels in 15px --text-primary 500 weight. Do NOT use a dropdown — tactile card choice is intentional.

Step 2 — Main Topic: Textarea label 'In one sentence, what's this about?' in 500 weight --text-primary. Helper text 'This will be visible to the other person…' in 14px --text-secondary. Character counter right-aligned 12px --text-tertiary; switches to --danger when >140 chars. No maxLength attribute — allow over-limit with visual warning per spec.

Steps 3+4 — Private fields (design doc §4.5): Lucide Lock icon (16px, 1.5px stroke) adjacent to label, in --text-secondary. Tooltip (shadcn/ui) on hover: 'Only you and the AI coach will see this.' Private field sections have --private-tint (#F0E9E0) left-border accent (4px) to visually reinforce privacy (design doc §1.3 Privacy is Visible).
Step 5 — Invitee name: plain Input, no privacy indicator (name is shared). 'Advanced' Collapsible section below in --text-tertiary 13px; solo mode checkbox inside styled subtly.

Crisis language: prominent banner (not inline) above the submit button. --danger-subtle background (#F2DCD8), --danger text, Lucide AlertTriangle. 'Not for crisis or emergency situations.' with link to crisis resources. Must be on every step or at minimum step 5.
Navigation buttons: Back = shadcn/ui Button variant='ghost'; Next/Submit = primary --accent filled. Single primary action per step (design doc §1.4).

Post-create invite screen (design doc §4.6): bg-canvas page, centered card bg-surface shadow-1. Invite URL in a read-only Input with 'Copy link' primary --accent button adjacent. Email/text share options as secondary ghost buttons. 'Start coaching now →' secondary link in --accent — visually subordinate to 'Copy link'.

Accessibility: fieldset + legend for radio card groups. Each Input/Textarea has an associated <label>. Step indicator uses aria-current='step' on active step. Form errors inline below inputs with role='alert'. Tooltip trigger is focusable with keyboard.

tasks.title: Case detail router and phase-appropriate view scaffold
tasks.summary: Implement CaseDetail.tsx at /cases/:caseId routing to phase-appropriate sub-routes by case.status.
acceptance_criteria: GET /cases/:caseId redirects to correct sub-route by case.status
Phase-skip redirects with toasts
PhaseHeader on all sub-routes
Loading: skeleton while cases/get in flight
caseId not found / not party: friendly error page
stack_practices: React Router v6 nested routes: CaseDetail.tsx is a layout route that renders <Outlet />.
Phase routing: useEffect + navigate with replace: true after cases/get resolves.
useQuery loading: undefined = loading → CaseDetailSkeleton; null = not found.
Anti-pattern: do NOT re-fetch cases/get in each sub-route — use useOutletContext.
design_practices: CaseDetail layout: PhaseHeader rendered above <Outlet /> in the layout — all sub-routes inherit it. Full-height layout with PhaseHeader fixed or sticky at top using bg-surface, border-bottom --border-default.
Loading skeleton: phase-appropriate placeholder (full-height column for chat views, centered card for ready-for-joint). Use shadcn/ui Skeleton with 300ms delay. --bg-surface-subtle shimmer.
Error page (not found / not party): centered on --bg-canvas. Friendly --text-secondary message, no technical jargon. Single 'Back to Dashboard' secondary link. No alarming colors.
Route transition: crossfade 100ms between sub-routes (design doc §2.6). No slide animations.

Accessibility: route change must move focus to the h1 of the new sub-route (useLocation subscription in App.tsx + h1 tabIndex={-1}). PhaseHeader <nav> landmark wraps the back link and phase title. Toast announcements use shadcn/ui toast's aria-live.

tasks.title: Private coaching view (/cases/:id/private) with crisis card integration
tasks.summary: Implement PrivateCoachingView.tsx: full-height chat, PrivacyBanner, mark-complete footer CTA + confirmation modal, CrisisCard integration.
acceptance_criteria: Loads privateCoaching/myMessages reactively
PrivacyBanner always visible
Streaming/Complete/Error message states
Send disabled while STREAMING
Mark-complete footer CTA + confirmation modal
Routes to /ready if case advances to READY_FOR_JOINT
CrisisCard appears on crisis keywords; dismissable; re-appears on new crisis message
stack_practices: Reactive streaming: useQuery(api.privateCoaching.myMessages) — no manual polling.
isStreaming: messages?.some(m => m.status === 'STREAMING'). Pass to MessageInput.
Route transition on case advance: watch caseData.case.status in useEffect.
CrisisCard key prop: reset dismissed state by changing key when new user message sent.
Mark-complete: shadcn/ui AlertDialog for confirmation.
design_practices: Full-height layout (design doc §4.7): PhaseHeader (sticky top), PrivacyBanner (persistent below header), ChatWindow (flex-grow, overflow-y-auto), MessageInput (fixed bottom), Footer CTA below input.

PrivacyBanner: full-width strip, --private-tint (#F0E9E0) background, Lucide Lock icon, 14px --text-secondary, always visible — not collapsible. Clicking opens shadcn/ui Dialog (shadow-3) with full privacy explanation.

Coach messages: --accent-subtle (#DCE7E0) background, left-aligned, Sparkles icon (Lucide 16px 1.5px stroke) at top-left. User messages: --bg-surface, right-aligned. Chat bubbles at 16px Inter for readability (design doc §2.2).

Streaming bubble: same --accent-subtle background with blinking cursor at end (500ms, step-end). No copy button while STREAMING. aria-label 'Coach is replying' on the STREAMING bubble's wrapper (role='status'). Remove role='status' when COMPLETE.

Mark-complete footer CTA (design doc §4.7): subtle treatment — text-based link or very small secondary button in --text-secondary, not a large prominent button. Design intent: don't rush users through private coaching. Footer area: bg-surface, border-top --border-default, 16px padding.

Mark-complete AlertDialog: shadcn/ui AlertDialog. Title: 'Ready to move on?' Body shows message count. Two actions: 'Continue Coaching' (cancel, ghost) and 'Mark Complete' (primary --accent). No celebration styling on confirmation.
CrisisCard: slides in below PrivacyBanner (150ms ease-out), --danger-subtle background, Lucide AlertTriangle in --danger. Dismissable ✕ button. Re-appears (via key prop reset) on new crisis message.

Accessibility: ChatWindow has role='log' aria-live='polite'. CrisisCard uses role='alert' so it's announced immediately. Mark-complete AlertDialog uses shadcn/ui's built-in focus trap and Esc close. Footer CTA keyboard-accessible.

tasks.title: Ready-for-joint view (/cases/:id/ready, synthesis display, enter CTA)
tasks.summary: Implement ReadyForJointView.tsx: synthesis card, 'Enter Joint Session' CTA calling jointChat/enter.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches jointChat/mySynthesis; skeleton while loading
Synthesis card with privacy label
Synthesis rendered as markdown
'Enter Joint Session →' primary button
Calls jointChat/enter; routes to /joint on success
Handles case already JOINT_ACTIVE
stack_practices: Markdown rendering: react-markdown with @tailwindcss/typography plugin for prose classes.
Idempotent enter: navigate('../joint') on any success from jointChat/enter.
Skeleton: synthesis === undefined → skeleton; synthesis === null → pending state.
Anti-pattern: do NOT render markdown via dangerouslySetInnerHTML — use react-markdown + rehype-sanitize.
design_practices: Page layout (design doc §4.8): centered column, max-width 720px, bg-canvas. Heading 'You've both completed private coaching.' in 500 weight --text-primary. Helper text in --text-secondary 15px. This screen is a moment of pause — ample vertical whitespace (48px between sections).

Synthesis card: bg-surface, shadow-1, border --border-default, 24px padding, border-radius 12px. Header strip: --private-tint background, Lock icon + 'Private to you — Jordan has their own version' in 13px --text-secondary.

Synthesis markdown rendering (design doc §4.8): use react-markdown with className='prose prose-sm max-w-none'. Typography plugin renders headings (**Areas of likely agreement**, **Points that will need real discussion**, **Suggested approach**) in 500 weight --text-primary. Body prose in --text-secondary 15px / 1.6 line-height.

'Enter Joint Session →' button (design doc §4.8): large primary --accent, centered, min-width 240px. This IS the main action — make it visually prominent. Helper text below: 'Jordan will see you've entered when they enter too.' in 13px --text-tertiary.

Synthesis loading/pending states: skeleton (300ms delay) while undefined. When null: 'Synthesis is being generated…' with a subtle shimmer or Pause icon — no spinner (too mechanical for the calm tone).

Accessibility: synthesis card content has aria-label='Your private guidance'. 'Enter Joint Session' button — after clicking, announce 'Entering joint session…' via a live region or button disabled state with loading text. Route change moves focus to /joint h1.

tasks.title: Joint chat view (/cases/:id/joint) with COST_LIMITED state handling
tasks.summary: Implement JointChatView.tsx: real-time reactive messages, party avatars, Coach messages, @-mention, closure banner, 'My guidance' Sheet, COST_LIMITED and read-only states.
acceptance_criteria: Reactive messages auto-update both clients
Party avatar colors correct; ⟡ glyph + coach-accent on Coach messages
isIntervention messages: warning-tint background
'Coach is thinking...' indicator for STREAMING Coach row
Closure proposal banner with Confirm/Reject buttons
'My guidance' Sheet with synthesisText
COST_LIMITED: banner + hidden Draft Coach + manual messages still work
CLOSED_*: input and Draft Coach hidden; 'case is closed' banner
stack_practices: Real-time: useQuery(api.jointChat.messages, { caseId }) — both clients share reactive subscription.
Party identification: msg.authorUserId === currentUserId.
Mobile sheet for Draft Coach: useMediaQuery('(max-width: 768px)') hook.
COST_LIMITED: caseData.case.status === 'COST_LIMITED' → show banner, hide DraftCoachPanel, keep MessageInput enabled.
Anti-pattern: do NOT have the client detect @mentions — mutation body handles it.
design_practices: Layout (design doc §4.9): max-width 1080px for joint chat (more breathing room). Desktop: single column message list, Draft Coach as 420px right panel (not part of the message list). Mobile <768px: Draft Coach as bottom Sheet full-screen.
Top nav (design doc §3.2): '← Back', case name, phase label, 'My guidance' button (ghost, Sparkles icon), 'Close' button (ghost, --text-secondary). No hamburger on desktop.

Party avatars (design doc §4.9): Alex/Initiator = #BFDBFE soft blue circle with initial; Jordan/Invitee = #FECDD3 soft rose; Coach = --coach-accent (#8B7AB5) at 20% opacity with --coach-accent initial/glyph. 32px circles. Consistent per party throughout the session.

Coach messages (design doc §4.9): left border 2px --coach-accent, bg --accent-subtle, ⟡ glyph prefix, --coach-accent text. isIntervention messages: swap --accent-subtle for a --warning (#B58B4D) tinted background to visually signal inflammatory-content response.
'Coach is thinking...' STREAMING state: inline indicator below last message, --text-secondary 13px italic. STREAMING Coach bubble has blinking cursor, role='status' aria-label='Coach is replying'.

Closure proposal banner (design doc §4.11): bg-surface, border --border-default, 16px padding, positioned above MessageInput. Icon: 📬 emoji or Lucide Mail. Two shadcn/ui buttons: 'Confirm' (primary --accent) and 'Reject and keep talking' (secondary). Banner must not block the message list — insert it between list and input.
'My guidance' Sheet: shadcn/ui Sheet side='right', width 480px on desktop. Header: 'My Guidance' 500 weight + Lock icon. Content: react-markdown synthesis text with prose classes. shadow-3 elevation.

COST_LIMITED banner: bg-warning (#B58B4D) at 10% opacity, border --warning, --warning text. 'AI Coach is temporarily unavailable. You can still message each other.' Hides DraftCoachPanel component entirely.
CLOSED_* state: 'case is closed' banner above the message list (not a modal). --bg-surface-subtle background. MessageInput hidden. Draft Coach hidden.

Accessibility: message list container role='log' aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions'. Each message authored by 'Coach' announced as 'Coach: [content]' via aria-label on the bubble. Closure banner uses role='alert' so both parties receive the notification announcement. 'My guidance' Sheet focus-trapped, Esc closes.

tasks.title: Draft Coach panel (side panel/bottom sheet, draft ready card, send gate)
tasks.summary: Implement DraftCoachPanel.tsx: 420px right panel on desktop, full-screen Sheet on mobile. Draft conversation, 'Draft it for me' button, DraftReadyCard with Send/Edit/Refine/Discard actions.
acceptance_criteria: Opens on '✨ Draft with Coach' click, calls draftCoach/startSession
Private label in panel
ChatWindow connected to draftCoach/sendMessage
'Draft it for me' posts canonical message
DraftReadyCard when finalDraft populated: Send/Edit/Refine/Discard
'Send this message': calls sendFinalDraft; panel closes; message in joint chat
'Edit before sending': transfers to main input; does NOT auto-send
Esc closes panel; focus returns to joint-chat input
Focus moves to draft textarea when panel opens
stack_practices: Panel layout: absolute/fixed 420px right panel on desktop; shadcn/ui Sheet side='bottom' className='h-screen' on mobile.
Focus management: useEffect → textarea focus when isOpen. useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>.
Esc close + focus return: onKeyDown on panel container. Pass jointChatInputRef as prop.
'Edit before sending': onTransferDraft(finalDraft) state setter prop from JointChatView.
Anti-pattern: do NOT auto-send when AI produces finalDraft — DraftReadyCard is a gate.
design_practices: Panel appearance (design doc §4.10): desktop — slides in from right (translateX 100% → 0, 200ms ease-out). 420px width, full viewport height, bg-surface, shadow-3 ('0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.10)'). z-index above message list, below Dialog modals.

Panel header (design doc §4.10): 'Draft Coach' title (500 weight --text-primary), Sparkles icon (Lucide 18px --coach-accent), Lock icon (Lucide 16px --text-secondary) persistent, ✕ close button (ghost, aria-label='Close Draft Coach').

Privacy label inside panel: small strip below header, --private-tint background, 13px --text-secondary: 'This is private to you. Jordan can't see what you're discussing here.' Hover on lock icon: tooltip 'Only the final message you send goes to the joint chat.'
Draft conversation: reuses ChatWindow component with COACH bubbles in --coach-accent tint and USER bubbles in --bg-surface. 16px bubble text for readability.

'Draft it for me' button: shadcn/ui Button variant='outline' full-width at bottom of conversation, Sparkles icon prefix. --text-primary, border --border-default. NOT primary — it is an option, not the mandate.

DraftReadyCard (design doc §4.10): bg-surface border --border-default shadow-1, 16px padding, 8px radius. 'Here's a draft based on what we talked about:' in 14px --text-secondary above the draft text block. Draft text block: bg-surface-subtle, 14px --text-primary, 16px padding, border-radius 8px, border --border-default.

DraftReadyCard action hierarchy (design doc §4.10): 'Send this message' = primary --accent filled, full-width. 'Edit before sending' = secondary outlined. 'Keep refining with Coach' = ghost. 'Discard' = ghost text-danger. Stack vertically. One primary action (design doc §1.4).
Mobile Sheet: shadcn/ui Sheet side='bottom' h-screen. Same header/content layout. Slides up from bottom (translateY, 200ms ease-out).

Accessibility: panel is not a Dialog — it does NOT trap focus globally (user can still interact with joint chat behind it). Draft textarea has aria-label='Message to Draft Coach'. DraftReadyCard 'Send this message' has aria-describedby pointing to the draft text block. Esc key closes and returns focus to joint-chat MessageInput.

tasks.title: Closure flow UI and closed case view (/cases/:id/closed)
tasks.summary: Implement closure modal (Resolved/Not resolved/Take a break), rejection flow, and /cases/:id/closed read-only archive view with tabs.
acceptance_criteria: Closure modal: three options with correct mutations
Resolved: summary textarea required (min 10 chars); calls proposeClosure
Not resolved: warning styling; calls unilateralClose
Take a break: closes modal, no state change
Rejection: rejectClosure; clears banner
Confirmation: confirmClosure; both parties route to /closed (reactive)
/cases/:id/closed: outcome label, closure summary, read-only tabs
Input and Draft Coach absent in closed view
stack_practices: Closure modal: shadcn/ui Dialog with custom content and three option buttons.
Tab component: shadcn/ui Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent. Default tab: 'Joint Chat'.
Reactive route update to /closed: watch caseData.case.status in JointChatView useEffect.
Summary textarea validation: isValid = closureSummary.trim().length >= 10.
Anti-pattern: do NOT render the other party's private coaching tab in closed view.
design_practices: Closure modal (design doc §4.11): shadcn/ui Dialog, shadow-3 elevation. Title: 'How would you like to close this case?' Header in 500 weight --text-primary. Three option blocks styled as cards with border --border-default, 16px padding. Hover: bg-surface-subtle, 150ms ease-out.

'Resolved' option: --accent accent-left-border (4px) on selected card. Textarea 'Briefly describe what you agreed to.' (5 rows, border --border-strong). Character count: right-aligned 12px --text-tertiary. 'Propose Resolution' = primary --accent, disabled until textarea.length >= 10.

'Not resolved' option: warning treatment — border-left 4px --danger, card bg subtle danger tint on select. 'Close without resolution' button = shadcn/ui Button variant='destructive' styled with --danger. 'This closes the case immediately for both of you.' in --text-secondary.
'Take a break' option: ghost/neutral card. 'Close' button = secondary outlined --text-secondary. No state change.

Closed case view (design doc §4.12): bg-canvas page. Header: case name, category pill, closure date in --text-secondary, outcome badge (Resolved = --accent, Not Resolved = --danger, Abandoned = --text-tertiary). Outcome badge: 12px text, bg-surface-subtle, rounded-full, px-2 py-1.
Closure summary (when Resolved): bg-accent-subtle (#DCE7E0), 16px padding, border-radius 8px, ShieldCheck icon, --text-primary 15px prose. Prominent placement below header.

Closed case tabs: shadcn/ui Tabs. TabsList with three triggers: 'Joint Chat' | 'My Private Coaching' | 'My Guidance'. Active trigger: --accent border-bottom. ChatWindow in readOnly mode (no MessageInput, no Draft Coach button).
'This case is closed' banner: bg-surface-subtle, border-bottom --border-default, 12px --text-secondary, centered. Full-width between PhaseHeader and tab content. NO input, NO Draft Coach.

Accessibility: closure Dialog focus-trapped, Esc closes (shadcn/ui handles). Tabs use correct ARIA tab pattern (role='tablist', role='tab', role='tabpanel') via shadcn/ui. Closed state read-only ChatWindow: message list still has role='log' but aria-live removed (no new messages expected).

tasks.title: Solo mode UI (PartyToggle, useSoloActingParty hook, all views integrated)
tasks.summary: Implement PartyToggle and useSoloActingParty hook with URL param state. Integrate into all data queries.
acceptance_criteria: PartyToggle: segmented control; only rendered when case.isSolo=true
Toggle state in ?as=initiator|invitee URL param; preserved on refresh
useSoloActingParty(): returns role for selected party
All queries respect toggle for solo mode
Both synthesisTexts differ in solo mode
PartyToggle absent when case.isSolo=false
Solo cases show 'Solo test' badge on dashboard and PhaseHeader
stack_practices: URL search param: useSearchParams from React Router v6. actingAs defaults to 'initiator'.
useSoloActingParty: reads URL param, returns 'INITIATOR' | 'INVITEE'.
PartyToggle: two buttons as segmented control, coach-accent for active state.
Anti-pattern: do NOT store solo party selection in localStorage — use URL param.
design_practices: PartyToggle (design doc §3.2): segmented control in PhaseHeader, right-aligned. Two segments: 'Viewing as: [Initiator Name]' and '[Invitee Name]'. Active segment: bg-coach-accent (#8B7AB5) at 100% opacity, white text, border-radius 6px. Inactive: --bg-surface-subtle, --text-secondary. Container: bg-surface, border --border-default, border-radius 8px, 4px padding. Only rendered when case.isSolo === true — absent otherwise (design doc §9.3).
PartyToggle transition: switching parties = 150ms bg crossfade ease-out. The toggle is 'prominent' (design doc §3.2) to make solo context unmistakable.

'Solo test' badge (design doc): text-xs Inter, bg-coach-accent at 20% opacity, text-coach-accent, px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full. Appears in dashboard case rows and PhaseHeader when case.isSolo. Short text: 'Solo'.

Visual differentiation: in solo mode, the PhaseHeader should visually signal the active party — consider a subtle left border or background tint in the party's avatar color to reinforce which perspective is active.

Accessibility: PartyToggle buttons use role='radio' / aria-checked pattern, or role='tab', aria-selected. Group has aria-label='Viewing as party'. Toggle state changes announce via aria-live='polite' region: 'Now viewing as Initiator.' URL param update does not itself announce — use the live region.

tasks.title: Profile page and logout
tasks.summary: Implement /profile: display name edit, sign-out.
acceptance_criteria: /profile: read-only email, editable displayName
Saving calls users/updateDisplayName; success toast
'Sign out' calls Convex Auth signOut; redirects to /
Logged-out access to /profile redirects to /login
Profile accessible from top nav
stack_practices: Sign out: `const { signOut } = useAuthActions(); await signOut()` then navigate('/').
Display name mutation: useMutation with optimistic UI.
Current user: useQuery(api.users.getCurrentUser).
Anti-pattern: do NOT expose non-editable fields (email, role, createdAt) as form fields.
design_practices: Page layout: centered card, max-width ~480px, bg-surface shadow-1, 32px padding. Background --bg-canvas. Simple single-column form — not a settings panel with sidebar.
Email field: read-only Input, --text-secondary text, bg-surface-subtle background to signal non-editable. Label 'Email address' in 14px 500 weight.
Display name Input: border --border-strong, 15px Inter, --text-primary. Save button: primary --accent, 'Save changes'. Disabled until name has changed from current value.
Success toast: shadcn/ui toast, neutral (not green celebration), 'Display name updated.' --text-primary. Auto-dismisses in 3s.
'Sign out' button: shadcn/ui Button variant='ghost' or outline, --text-secondary, at bottom of card with top border divider --border-default. NOT a danger button — signing out is routine.
Top nav link to /profile: user avatar circle (32px, initials, --accent bg) in top-right corner of the global nav. Clicking opens profile route.

Accessibility: form has accessible <label> for each field. Read-only email uses aria-readonly='true'. Save button disabled state: aria-disabled='true'. Success toast announced via shadcn/ui's built-in aria-live.

tasks.title: Admin template UI (list, edit with version history, audit log)
tasks.summary: Implement admin-only routes: /admin/templates, /admin/templates/:id, /admin/audit.
acceptance_criteria: Non-admins get 403 page (server-side role check)
TemplatesListPage: table with relevant columns; [+ New Template] button
Pinned Cases Count computed via query
TemplateEditPage: two-pane form + version history; 'Publish New Version' button
Version comparison modal: side-by-side text view
'Archive Template': confirmation with pinned case count warning
AuditLogPage: filterable table; row click opens Drawer with metadata JSON
Audit log read-only
stack_practices: Admin route guard: RequireAdmin wrapper reads currentUser.role === 'ADMIN'.
Table: shadcn/ui Table, TableHeader, TableRow, TableHead, TableBody, TableCell.
Version comparison modal: shadcn/ui Dialog with two side-by-side <pre> blocks.
Drawer for audit log: shadcn/ui Sheet side='right'.
Anti-pattern: do NOT bypass server-side assertAdmin check.
design_practices: 403 page: centered on --bg-canvas. '403 — Access Restricted' heading 500 weight --text-primary. Body in --text-secondary. 'Back to Dashboard' secondary link. Calm, not alarming — no red banners.

Templates list table (design doc §4.13): shadcn/ui Table. bg-canvas page, bg-surface table container with shadow-1. Column headers in 13px 500 weight --text-secondary uppercase. Rows in 14px --text-primary. Hover row: bg-surface-subtle, 150ms ease-out. Status badge (Active / Archived): Active = --accent text + bg-accent-subtle; Archived = --text-tertiary + bg-surface-subtle. Pinned Cases Count column: 14px --text-secondary.
'+ New Template' button: primary --accent, top-right of page. Single primary action (design doc §1.4).

Template edit page (design doc §4.13): two-pane layout. Left pane (~60% width): form with shadcn/ui Textarea for Global Guidance, Coach Instructions, Draft Coach Instructions (all markdown, 8+ rows). Labels in 14px 500 weight. Right pane (~40%): version history timeline — vertical list of published versions with date, admin name, notes in 13px --text-secondary. Each version has a 'View' ghost button.
Version history timeline: vertical connector line in --border-default. Each version node: 8px circle --accent, date label --text-tertiary 12px, 'View' ghost button.

'Publish New Version' button: primary --accent, full-width at bottom of left pane. 'Archive Template' button: shadcn/ui Button variant with --danger color, at bottom, below a border divider. Archive confirmation Dialog shows pinned case count in --warning color.

Version comparison modal: shadcn/ui Dialog, wide (max-width 800px), shadow-3. Two columns: 'Version N-1' and 'Version N' headings in 500 weight. Content in <pre> or monospace textarea, JetBrains Mono 13px, --text-secondary. bg-surface-subtle background.

Audit log page (design doc §4.13): shadcn/ui Table. Filter input (shadcn/ui Input) for actor email above table. Actor | Action | Target | Timestamp columns. Timestamp in JetBrains Mono 13px --text-tertiary. Row click → shadcn/ui Sheet side='right' with JSON metadata in <pre className='text-xs overflow-auto font-mono'>.

Accessibility: admin tables use proper <th scope='col'> headers. Filter input has <label> or aria-label='Filter by actor email'. Audit log Sheet focus-trapped, Esc closes. All icon-only buttons in version history have aria-label.

tasks.title: Accessibility pass (WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard navigation, screen reader, reduced motion)
tasks.summary: Systematic accessibility audit and remediation across all 12+ routes.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly one <main> per page; <nav> and <aside> landmarks
Chat regions: role='log', aria-live='polite'
Streaming messages announce 'Coach is replying' once per response
All modals trap focus; Esc closes; focus returns to trigger
Route change moves focus to h1
Draft Coach panel: focus moves to textarea on open
prefers-reduced-motion: all animations disabled
All text ≥ 4.5:1 contrast in light and dark mode
Keyboard-only critical path test passable
All buttons have visible text or aria-label
stack_practices: axe-core audit: run @axe-core/react in development mode via main.tsx (dev-only).
Focus trap: shadcn/ui Dialog and Sheet via @radix-ui/react-focus-scope — do NOT reimplement.
Route change focus: useLocation subscription in App.tsx; document.querySelector('h1')?.focus().
aria-live for chat: role='log' aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions' on message list.
Focus ring: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; } globally.
Contrast check: use are-you-ok CLI or browser DevTools.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use outline: none without a :focus-visible replacement.
design_practices: Color contrast audit: check every --text-* on every --bg-* combination using design doc §2.1 tokens. All must meet WCAG 2.1 AA 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text (18px+ or 14px+ bold). Check both light and dark mode. Critical pairs to verify: --text-secondary on --bg-canvas, --text-tertiary on --bg-surface, --accent on --bg-surface, --danger on --bg-canvas.

Focus ring: global :focus-visible rule using var(--accent) (#6B8E7F) 2px solid, 2px offset. Apply outline-none ONLY to elements that provide a custom :focus-visible replacement (e.g., custom active state styling). Never remove outline entirely.

prefers-reduced-motion: T18 sets all transition durations to 0ms and pauses CSS animations. T33 verifies this is consistently applied: message arrival animation, streaming cursor blink, panel slide-in, route crossfade, skeleton shimmer — ALL must cease. Use @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) guards.

Landmark structure (design doc §9.5): exactly one <main> per page (not per route component). <nav> for PhaseHeader navigation. <aside> for Draft Coach panel (it is complementary content). Verify with @axe-core/react violations list.

Chat accessibility (design doc §9.5): role='log' aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions' on ChatWindow container. STREAMING Coach bubble: role='status' aria-label='Coach is replying' — announced once. COMPLETE: remove role='status'. New user messages are NOT announced (they are authored by the user, not new arrivals).

Modal / Sheet accessibility: shadcn/ui Dialog and Sheet use Radix UI FocusScope and aria-modal='true'. Esc must close. Focus must return to the trigger element (button that opened it) on close. Verify this for: PrivacyBanner dialog, MarkComplete AlertDialog, CrisisCard (not a modal — no focus trap), DraftCoachPanel (Sheet), closure modal, version comparison modal, audit log drawer.

Route change focus: after each React Router navigation, programmatically focus the page's h1 (tabIndex={-1} on h1). This is critical for screen reader users who would otherwise be lost at the previous scroll position.

Keyboard critical path (design doc §9.5): Tab through landing → login → dashboard → new case (all 5 steps) → invite page → private coaching (send message, mark complete) → ready for joint (enter) → joint chat (send message, open Draft Coach, send draft, propose closure). All actions completable without mouse.

tasks.title: Playwright E2E test suite (all 7 spec files, multi-browser)
tasks.summary: Implement the full Playwright test suite: 7 spec files, multi-browser projects, CLAUDE_MOCK=true stub.
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE_MOCK=true: deterministic stub with configurable delays; no live Anthropic calls
All 7 spec files pass 100% with CLAUDE_MOCK=true
Three browser projects; axe-core scan in solo-full-flow
stack_practices: Playwright config: three projects (chromium, firefox, webkit).
CLAUDE_MOCK stub: check process.env.CLAUDE_MOCK === 'true' at top of each AI action.
Two-context invite test: browser.newContext() per user.
axe-core in Playwright: @axe-core/playwright after each major navigation.
Anti-pattern: do NOT use page.waitForTimeout — use page.waitForSelector or expect(locator).toBeVisible().

tasks.title: Vitest unit tests for core utility modules
tasks.summary: Vitest unit tests covering privacy filter, prompt assembly, transcript compression, invite token generation, state machine transitions.
acceptance_criteria: privacyFilter: ≥5 adversarial, ≥3 benign, boundary cases
All 4 assemblePrompt roles tested
All 9 valid state transitions and ≥8 illegal transitions tested
All tests pass with `npx vitest run`
stack_practices: Test files co-located in convex/lib/__tests__/.
Vitest config: environment: 'node'.
Mock for transcriptCompression: vi.fn() for Anthropic client.
Anti-pattern: do NOT import convex/_generated/server in Vitest tests.

tasks.title: CI pipeline (GitHub Actions: lint, typecheck, unit, E2E)
tasks.summary: Configure .github/workflows/ci.yml with four jobs: lint, typecheck, unit, e2e.
acceptance_criteria: Four jobs on push to PR branches targeting main
lint, typecheck, unit pass independently
e2e: deploy to Convex dev; seed; Playwright with CLAUDE_MOCK=true; multi-browser
node_modules cached
stack_practices: GitHub Actions cache: actions/cache@v4 with npm ci (not npm install).
Convex deploy in CI: npx convex deploy using CONVEX_DEPLOY_KEY secret.
Playwright in CI: npx playwright install --with-deps.
Anti-pattern: do NOT run npx convex dev in CI — use npx convex deploy.
